response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile senior engineer (15+ years, strong TDD/craftsmanship culture, some high-throughput systems exposure at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic), but the profile does not match a Principal IC6 growth/experimentation role. There is no evidence of building experimentation platforms, feature-flagging systems, telemetry/metrics infrastructure, or statistical analysis workflows, and no demonstrated multi-workstream technical leadership at enterprise scale. Career history is mostly senior-level IC work at small-to-mid startups, which falls short of the principal-level scope and the domain-specific requirements.
strengths: 15+ years of broad engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and backend systems
High-scale, low-latency systems experience (micro-services handling millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby)
Strong engineering fundamentals: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, open-source authorship, and teaching/mentoring
concerns: No experimentation, A/B testing, feature-flagging, or growth-measurement platform experience
No demonstrated principal-level scope: no multi-team initiatives, platform ownership, or org-wide architectural leadership
Limited evidence of data/statistics aptitude for metrics design, bias detection, or experimental analysis

key_factors.factor: Seniority gap vs Principal IC6 scope
Solid engineering fundamentals and distributed-systems exposure
